challenges.title: Grade Classifier
challenges.description: Write an if/elif/else chain that takes a variable `score` (set to 85) and sets `grade` to the appropriate letter grade: 'A' for 90 and above, 'B' for 80 and above, 'C' for 70 and above, 'D' for 60 and above, or 'F' for anything below 60.
challenges.starter_code:
score = 85

# Write your if/elif/else chain here to set `grade`
hints: Start with the highest threshold (>= 90) and work down.
Remember that elif checks are only reached if all previous conditions were False.
The else clause catches everything below 60.

challenges.title: Even/Odd Checker
challenges.description: Given `number = 17`, write an if/else statement that sets `parity` to the string 'even' if the number is even, or 'odd' if it is odd. Use the modulo operator (%) to determine divisibility by 2.
challenges.starter_code:
number = 17

# Write your if/else here to set `parity`
challenges.test_code:
assert number == 17, 'number should remain 17'
assert parity == 'odd', f'Expected odd for 17, got {parity}'

# Verify the logic works for other values
test_cases = [(0, 'even'), (1, 'odd'), (2, 'even'), (17, 'odd'), (100, 'even'), (-3, 'odd'), (-4, 'even')]
for n, expected in test_cases:
    result = 'even' if n % 2 == 0 else 'odd'
    assert result == expected, f'For {n}, expected {expected} but got {result}'
hints: The modulo operator (%) returns the remainder of division.
A number is even if number % 2 == 0.
You only need if and else — no elif required.
